title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. racemosa
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. racemosa, also known as the Scottish harebell, is a flowering plant species within the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its delicate blue-purple flowers and bell-shaped calyx. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Campanula rotundifolia subsp. racemosa include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Campanula rotundifolia subsp. racemosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties.
